Found it. Here's what the search turned up:

- **Linear — JOE-7: "Add rainbow colored snake surprise"** (Backlog, Snake Game Project). The snake should occasionally be rainbow colored — rare, roughly 1 in 100 games. When it's a rainbow snake game, show a banner saying "Rainbow Snake" in rainbow colors.
- **Notion** — nothing relevant (only a generic "Getting Started" help page came back).

Since plan mode is on, I'll research the codebase so I can plan how to build JOE-7. Let me explore the relevant areas in parallel.

I've kicked off exploration of the codebase to plan **JOE-7 (Rainbow Snake)**. Two agents are mapping (1) snake rendering + per-game init (where to hook the ~1-in-100 roll) and (2) existing banner/transition UI patterns to reuse for the "Rainbow Snake" banner.

I'll synthesize a concrete implementation plan once they report back.

<result>I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is my report.

---

# Snake Game — Feature Exploration for JOE-7 "Rainbow Snake Surprise"

## Rendering technology

**HTML5 Canvas 2D**, rendered as a rotating **isometric** scene (no WebGL, no DOM/SVG — confirmed in README and code). Each snake segment is drawn as an extruded 3D "prism" block. Colors are supplied as a **3-tuple `[topColor, rightColor, leftColor]`** (the three visible faces of each block), each a hex string, applied via `ctx.fillStyle`.

## Key files

- `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts` (2592 lines) — the real game, class `SnakeGame`. **This is your target.**
- `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/menu-demo.ts` — a separate, self-contained demo snake for the menu screen (its own `draw()` and color constants). Not the actual game; only touch if you also want rainbow in the menu demo.
- `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/iron-snake.ts` — board-mask generation; home of the reusable RNG type/helper.
- `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/game-types.ts` — `Position`, `Direction`, `GameMode` types.

## 1. How/where the snake is rendered and where color is set

Main render method: **`SnakeGame.draw()` at `main.ts:703`**.

Flow within `draw()`:
- `main.ts:719-721` — iterates `this.snake` and pushes render objects tagged `'head' | 'body' | 'tail'`. **Note: the object records only `{x, y, type}` — no segment index** (relevant for per-segment rainbow; see implementation note below).
- `main.ts:766-772` — **where snake colors are currently defined** (inline literals, computed fresh every frame):
  - P1 head: `['#4ade80', '#22c55e', '#16a34a']`, P1 body: `['#22c55e', '#16a34a', '#15803d']` (green)
  - P2 head: `['#60a5fa', '#3b82f6', '#2563eb']`, P2 body: `['#3b82f6', '#2563eb', '#1d4ed8']` (blue)
  - A local `tint()` helper blends these toward the hunger/starvation warning palette (`HUNGER_HEAD_COLORS`/`HUNGER_BODY_COLORS`, defined `main.ts:51-52`).
- `main.ts:780-807` — the render switch that draws each segment by type, passing the color tuple to the draw helpers:
  - `case 'head'` → `drawSnakeHead(obj.x, obj.y, head1, this.direction)`
  - `case 'body'` → `drawBlock(obj.x, obj.y, body1[0], body1[1], body1[2])`
  - `case 'tail'` → `drawSnakeTail(obj.x, obj.y, body1, ...)`

Low-level draw helpers (all take the `colors` tuple and ultimately set `ctx.fillStyle`):
- `drawBlock(gx, gy, topColor, rightColor, leftColor, heightScale=1)` — `main.ts:490`
- `drawPrism(c, bh, topColor, rightColor, leftColor)` — `main.ts:507` (the actual `ctx.fillStyle`/`ctx.fill()` calls: top face `main.ts:554`, side faces `main.ts:529` & `543`)
- `drawSnakeHead(gx, gy, colors, dir)` — `main.ts:565`
- `drawSnakeTail(gx, gy, colors, awayDir)` — `main.ts:617`

Color-interpolation helper you can reuse for building a gradient/rainbow: **`lerpHex(from, to, t)` at `main.ts:1819`** (interpolates two hex colors).

## 2. Where a new game is initialized (hook point for the 1-in-100 roll)

**`SnakeGame.startNewGame(mode?)` at `main.ts:1223`** (ends `main.ts:1329`). This runs exactly once when a game begins (wired to the New Game / Two Player / Bot-vs-Bot / Play Again buttons at `main.ts:831-856`, and Enter/reset handlers). It resets all per-game state (score, snake positions, level, speed, hunger counters, death state) at `main.ts:1243-1303`.

This is the ideal place to roll the surprise once and persist it for the whole game, e.g. `this.rainbowSnake = Math.random() < 0.01`.

**Where per-game state lives:** private class fields declared at the top of `SnakeGame`, roughly `main.ts:89-140` (e.g. `snake`, `score`, `level`, `gameSpeed` at 89-123; bot flags at 125-140). Add a new field such as `private rainbowSnake = false` alongside these, set it in `startNewGame()`, and read it in `draw()` to choose the color tuple.

## 3. How colors are applied to segments (implementation note)

Colors are per-face hex strings passed as `[top, right, left]` tuples into `drawBlock`/`drawSnakeHead`/`drawSnakeTail`. To make the snake rainbow **per segment**, you'll need the segment's index at draw time, which the current `objects` array (`main.ts:714`, pushed at `719-721`) does **not** carry — it only has `{x, y, type}`. Two options:
- Add an `index` (and length) field to the pushed objects at `main.ts:719-721`, then in the switch (`main.ts:780-807`) compute a color from the index (e.g. `hsl` hue = `index / snake.length * 360`, converted to hex, and shade the three faces).
- Or gate at `main.ts:769-772`: when `this.rainbowSnake`, generate colors from the segment index instead of the fixed green tuple.

`hsl(...)` strings are valid `ctx.fillStyle` values, so you can skip hex conversion for the top face; but the face-shading model expects three distinct hex shades per block, so generating three HSL lightness variants (or using `lerpHex` toward black/white) will match the existing 3D look.

## 4. Existing randomness / RNG utilities to reuse

- The project uses plain **`Math.random()`** everywhere; there is no seeded global RNG.
- **Food placement** (closest analog to your roll): `main.ts:2173` (`emptyCells[Math.floor(Math.random() * emptyCells.length)]`) and fallback at `main.ts:2182-2183`, inside `spawnFood()`.
- Reusable helper: **`randInt(rng, lo, hi)` at `iron-snake.ts:62`** — but it is **not exported** (module-private). The exported entry points `generateIronSnakeBoard(gridSize, rng = Math.random)` (`iron-snake.ts:311`) and the `Rng` type accept an injectable RNG defaulting to `Math.random`.
- The menu demo also uses `Math.random()` directly (`menu-demo.ts:72, 127, 294`).

For a simple "1 in 100" roll, `Math.random() < 0.01` in `startNewGame()` matches the codebase's existing conventions — no need to pull in `iron-snake.ts`'s `randInt` (it isn't exported anyway).</result>

<result>I have everything needed. Here is my report on the UI patterns relevant to building the JOE-7 "Rainbow Snake" banner.

## Summary

The game renders **all text as DOM/CSS elements** (never Canvas `fillText`). The Canvas is used only for the isometric board, snake, and food geometry. For your "Rainbow Snake" banner, the cleanest reuse is the existing **`.death-banner` / `.starvation-warning` overlay pattern** — an absolutely-positioned element inside `.canvas-wrap` that overlays the board without shifting layout, toggled via a `hidden` class and a CSS `@keyframes` animation. There is currently **no rainbow/gradient/hue-cycling styling anywhere** — you would be adding the first.

---

## 1. Existing banners, overlays, toasts, and on-screen text

All defined as DOM in `index.html`, styled in `src/style.css`, and controlled in `src/main.ts`.

**Canvas-overlay banners (best template for Rainbow Snake):** these live inside `.canvas-wrap` (which is `position: relative`, `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/style.css:170-173`) so an `position:absolute` child floats over the board.

- **Death banner** — DOM `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/index.html:93` (`<div id="death-banner" class="death-banner hidden" role="alert">`). Styled `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/style.css:202-225`. Shown/hidden in `showDeathBanner()` / `hideDeathBanner()` at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:2247-2255`:
  ```ts
  private showDeathBanner() {
    const bannerEl = document.getElementById('death-banner')!
    bannerEl.textContent = `${this.deathMessage} — Press any key`
    bannerEl.classList.remove('hidden')
  }
  ```
- **Starvation warning** (a pulsing toast) — DOM `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/index.html:92`, styled `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/style.css:176-198`, controlled by `updateStarvationWarning()` at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:2222-2242`.

**HUD text (score / level / goal):** `.game-info` block `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/index.html:81-89`, styled `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/style.css:227-237`. Updated in `updateUI()` at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:2194-2216` (e.g. `document.getElementById('level')!.textContent = this.level.toString()` at line 2201). Note there is **no "level up" announcement banner** — the level only changes in this HUD number.

**Full-screen text screens:** Game-over screen `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/index.html:97-119` (title `#game-over-title`, `.winner-text` styled at `src/style.css:265-269`, `.final-score`); Pause overlay `#pause.overlay` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/index.html:120-123` styled via `.screen.overlay` at `src/style.css:105-114`; Menu title `.menu-title` at `src/style.css:73-78`.

---

## 2. Level / state transitions (JOE-5 smooth transition, Iron Snake)

The "smooth transition from level to level" (commit `ee8ce65`, JOE-5) is a **Canvas board-shape morph, not a text banner**. There is no transition text/banner to reuse — the animation reshapes the playable board cells. All in `src/main.ts`:

- `advanceLevel()` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:1928-1944` — snapshots the old mask, calls `applyLevelAdvance()`, pauses the logic loop, and kicks off the morph.
- `applyLevelAdvance()` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:1949` onward — mutates level/board/goal/speed state.
- `startLevelMorph()` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts` (~2020-2082) — builds a centre-out "wave" of changed cells and starts a `requestAnimationFrame` loop.
- `morphStep(timestamp)` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:2084-2112` — reveals cells in discrete timed steps, re-`draw()`ing only when a step changes.
- `finishLevelMorph()` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:2115-2134` and `cancelLevelMorph()` `:2138-2151`.
- Transition state fields: `isTransitioning`, `transitionRafId`, `morphWork`, `morphSteps`, `morphStepMs`, etc. at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:203-214`. Timing constants `LEVEL_MORPH_*` at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:35-38`. Board regeneration helper `growIronSnakeBoard` lives in `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/iron-snake.ts`.

---

## 3. How text is drawn + styling/animation approach

- **Text: 100% DOM/CSS.** A search for `fillText`/`strokeText`/`ctx.font` across `src/` returns nothing — the Canvas (`draw()`, `drawBlock`, `drawSnakeHead`, `drawIsoCube` region around `src/main.ts:340-620` and `:740-807`) draws only geometry. So the Rainbow Snake banner should be a DOM element, matching the existing banners.
- **CSS `@keyframes` animations** (for DOM banners): `starve-pulse` `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/style.css:195-198`, `death-pulse` `:222-225`, `expand-panel-fade` `:386-389`. These are opacity/box-shadow pulses applied via `animation:` on the element (e.g. `src/style.css:192`, `:219`).
- **`requestAnimationFrame`** (for Canvas animations only): board auto-rotation `autoRotateStep` (`src/main.ts:646`, `:698`), the level morph (`src/main.ts:2081`, `:2111`), and the menu demo (`src/menu-demo.ts:82`, `:120`).
- Show/hide is universally the `.hidden { display: none }` class (`src/style.css:253-255`) toggled via `classList`.

---

## 4. Existing rainbow / multi-color / gradient styling

**None.** Grep for `rainbow|hue|hsl|conic|linear-gradient` found only:

- One `radial-gradient` used purely as a **mask** (fade-out vignette) on the menu demo canvas — `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/style.css:44-45`. Not a color effect.

There is **no hue-cycling, no conic/linear color gradient, no HSL usage** anywhere. Snake/food colors are hardcoded hex triples for the three isometric faces (top/right/left), e.g. green player at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:769-770`, blue player `:771-772`. The only color-transition mechanism is `lerpHex(from, to, t)` at `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts:1819`, used by the hunger-tint blend (`tint(...)` at `src/main.ts:766-772`). So a rainbow effect (CSS gradient text, or an HSL hue sweep across snake segments) would be net-new — `lerpHex` and the per-segment `drawBlock(x, y, top, right, left)` calls (`src/main.ts:786`, `:795`) are the hooks if you want an actual rainbow-colored snake body rather than just the banner.

---

## Recommendation for JOE-7

For the banner: add a `<div id="rainbow-banner" class="rainbow-banner hidden">Rainbow Snake</div>` inside `.canvas-wrap` (`index.html:90-94`, next to `#death-banner`), mirror the `.death-banner` CSS block (`src/style.css:202-225`) but swap the solid color for a rainbow `background: linear-gradient(...)` (or animated hue via a new `@keyframes`) with `background-clip: text`, and add `showRainbowBanner()`/`hideRainbowBanner()` methods modeled on `showDeathBanner()` (`src/main.ts:2247-2255`). For an actual rainbow-colored snake, hook the per-segment color at the `drawBlock` body cases (`src/main.ts:785-798`), generating HSL colors per index.</result>

You're away, so I'll proceed with sensible defaults and note them clearly so you can adjust: **animated shimmer** (matches your documented preference for visible, gameplay-paced motion) and an **auto-dismiss (~3s) banner**. Let me confirm the build/test commands, then write the plan.

I have everything needed. Writing the plan now.

Oh, that's a great flavor direction — the classic Mortal Kombat **"FRIENDSHIP!"** finisher: the cheerful rainbow arc, chunky arcade text, and a jarringly wholesome tonal flip. Let me fold that into the plan's banner design.

Plan updated and saved to `optimized-petting-donut.md`. Here's the shape of it:

## JOE-7 — Rainbow Snake (MK "FRIENDSHIP!" style)

**The roll (≈1 in 100).** Add `RAINBOW_CHANCE = 0.01` and a per-game `rainbowSnake` flag, rolled once in `startNewGame()` (`main.ts:1223`) so it holds for the whole game. A `?rainbow=1`/`?rainbow=0` URL override gives deterministic testing instead of waiting on a 1% chance.

**Rainbow snake body.** In `draw()` (`main.ts:703-807`), tag snake-1 segments with their index and color each by an HSL hue that sweeps along the body and **animates** (the board's existing rAF rotation loop already redraws every frame, so the shimmer is free). Scoped to player 1 — P2 stays blue in two-player so identity is preserved. Normal games are completely unchanged.

**The banner — the fun part.** Reuse the `.death-banner` overlay *mechanics*, but style it as the classic Mortal Kombat friendship reveal:
- a semicircular **rainbow arc** (CSS concentric radial-gradient bands) behind
- **"RAINBOW SNAKE"** in chunky all-caps arcade text — bright yellow, thick layered-`text-shadow` outline — that **pops/bounces in, flashes** like the MK caption, then auto-dismisses (~3s fade).

**Verification.** `npm run dev` → open `?rainbow=1`, start a game (rainbow snake + friendship banner), `?rainbow=0` (normal), confirm P2 stays blue. `npm run build` (typecheck) + `npm test` (Playwright) with a new spec asserting the banner on `?rainbow=1`.

Two things worth flagging:
- I defaulted the snake to an **animated** shimmer (matches your visible-motion preference); say the word if you'd rather it be a static head-to-tail gradient.
- An MK-style **announcer sound sting** is left OFF by default (no audio system exists yet; it'd be a small WebAudio jingle) — want it in?

## What I did

**Branch:** `josephhart/joe-7-add-rainbow-colored-snake-suprise` (I built it here in this session rather than a literally separate conversation).

**Changes** (uncommitted, ready for your review):
- **`src/main.ts`** — a `RAINBOW_CHANCE = 0.01` roll in `startNewGame()` that holds for the whole game; per-segment HSL coloring of the P1 snake in `draw()` (hue runs along the body and scrolls over time, animated by the existing per-frame redraw); `showRainbowBanner()`/`hideRainbowBanner()` with a flash-then-fade auto-dismiss; and a `?rainbow=1|0` URL override for deterministic testing.
- **`index.html`** + **`src/style.css`** — the Mortal Kombat "FRIENDSHIP!"-style banner: a CSS rainbow arc behind chunky "RAINBOW SNAKE" arcade text that pops in, flashes, then fades.
- **`tests/e2e/rainbow.spec.ts`** — new Playwright specs asserting the banner appears + auto-dismisses on `?rainbow=1` and never shows on `?rainbow=0`.

**Scope decision:** rainbow applies to player 1's snake; in two-player modes P2 stays blue so identity is preserved. Normal (non-rainbow) games are byte-for-byte unchanged.

## Verification
- `npm run build` — clean (tsc + vite).
- `npm test` — **10/10 pass** (8 existing + 2 new rainbow specs).
- Visual (screenshots above): the friendship banner renders correctly, and a bot-grown rainbow snake shows the full head-to-tail spectrum with the animating hue.

A couple of things I decided while you were away (easy to change):
- Snake is an **animated** shimmer (per your visible-motion preference), not a static gradient.
- **No announcer sound** — there's no audio system yet; I left it out. Want a small WebAudio "Friendship!" sting added?
